summary: Integrated ADR 0305 onto the latest realistic origin/main baseline by replaying the exact-main k6 smoke and load paths from committed source 0d6e8c9eb5d9d086e74cf92d8165248295baa076 after rebasing onto origin/main commit 5c7e07235f7b0da1f756148e145397f0ac6ceb10. The mainline closeout corrected a stale Prometheus private-bind drift on monitoring-lv3, re-verified passing Keycloak and OpenFGA smoke receipts from docker-build-lv3, and confirmed that the controller-side load path now finishes and preserves truthful receipts instead of hanging when notification publication is unavailable. The final load replay still recorded a real live-platform failure for Keycloak while OpenFGA passed the run but reported a depleted remaining error budget warning.

verification.observed: The ADR 0305 stack was rebased onto origin/main commit 5c7e07235f7b0da1f756148e145397f0ac6ceb10, which carried repository version 0.177.117 and platform version 0.130.76 before the release cut.

The first exact-main smoke attempt failed while Prometheus on monitoring-lv3 was still loopback-bound and OpenFGA was restarting on docker-runtime-lv3. After make converge-monitoring completed and the bind correction was documented, Prometheus listened on 0.0.0.0:9090 and the build host could reach http://10.10.10.40:9090/-/ready.

make k6-smoke from committed source produced receipts/k6/smoke-keycloak-20260331T133226Z.json and receipts/k6/smoke-openfga-20260331T133226Z.json. Keycloak recorded 110 requests with 0 failures and p95 95.03 ms; OpenFGA recorded 112 requests with 0 failures and p95 84.38 ms.

make k6-load completed from the same committed source without the earlier NATS deadlock and preserved receipts plus raw summary export. Keycloak recorded 1585 requests with 441 failures (27.82% error rate) and failed the declared 1% objective; OpenFGA recorded 1184 requests with 8 failures (0.68%) and passed the run while still reporting error_budget_remaining_pct 0.0.

The final controller-side load replay recorded warning-only notification failures when the local NATS endpoint nats://127.0.0.1:4222 refused connections and ntfy timed out, proving the bounded post-run path preserves truthful receipts instead of hanging or dropping the evidence.

make remote-validate passed on docker-build-lv3, and the final make pre-push-gate also passed after remote_exec.sh transparently fell back locally when the build host hit transient 502 Bad Gateway errors pulling registry.lv3.org/check-runner/infra:2026.03.23 for the packer-validate and tofu-validate lanes.

correction_loop.repair_action: Completed make converge-monitoring from the rebased exact-main tree and documented the final Prometheus bind correction so the service listened on 0.0.0.0:9090 instead of 127.0.0.1:9090.
correction_loop.verification: systemctl cat lv3-prometheus showed --web.listen-address=0.0.0.0:9090, ss -ltnp showed Prometheus listening on *:9090, and the build host reached http://10.10.10.40:9090/-/ready before the passing smoke rerun.

notes: This receipt records the exact-main integration closeout for ADR 0305; the first verified live platform implementation still occurred earlier on platform version 0.130.75.
Platform version remains 0.130.76 for this merge because the ADR was already live before the mainline closeout; repository version advances to 0.177.118.

The branch-local receipt trail under receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-31-ws-0305-* remains part of the audit history and was not discarded when the exact-main replay replaced the stale release candidate.

The final pre-push proof set intentionally preserves the transient Harbor-backed runner-image pull failure on docker-build-lv3 alongside the successful local-fallback gate so the branch documents both the repo pass and the remaining build-host registry wrinkle.
